fix: BTQM fullscreen canvas now fills stage — override width:auto (intrinsic 640px) with width:100% (#159)

Root cause: <canvas> is a CSS replaced element. The general overlay rule
`width: auto !important` resolves to the intrinsic `width` attribute value
(640 px), not the container width, so Phaser's Scale.FIT had no effect.

- #btqm-canvas: height:100% → height:auto so flex parent can centre it;
  width → min(100%, calc(max-height × 640/448)) to avoid overflow on
  short/wide viewports.
- #btqm-canvas canvas: add width:100% !important (fills container) +
  explicit height:auto !important; aspect-ratio:640/448 (set by existing
  overlay JS) then drives proportional height.
- Add @supports not dvh fallback for both new container rules.
- game page: extend game.scale.refresh() to 3 passes (150/300/600ms) so
  Phaser input canvasBounds update reliably after layout completes.

feat(ts-http-runtime): migrate to #platform/* subpath imports (#37974)

## Migrate `@typespec/ts-http-runtime` to `#platform/*` subpath imports

Replaces warp's `polyfillSuffix` mechanism with Node.js [subpath
imports](https://nodejs.org/api/packages.html#subpath-imports)
(`#platform/*`) for all platform-specific code in `ts-http-runtime`.

### Why

`polyfillSuffix` works by silently swapping files at build time (e.g.
`foo.ts` → `foo-browser.mts`) based on the warp target. This is
invisible to TypeScript — all platform variants share one set of type
declarations, so the compiler can't distinguish what's available on each
target. It also forces runtime environment sniffing for any logic that
needs to know its platform, which is fundamentally unreliable in hybrid
environments.

Node.js subpath imports solve both problems: each platform variant gets
its own types (caught at compile time), and the correct module is
selected by the package manager/bundler resolving `package.json`
conditions — no runtime guesswork.

Concrete benefits:

- **Type safety** — each platform variant is independently type-checked.
For example, the Node `isNodeReadableStream` returns `x is Readable`
(from `node:stream`), the browser version uses `instanceof
ReadableStream`, and RN returns `x is never`. The old approach silently
produced incorrect type narrowing on non-Node platforms.

- **Environment detection correctness** — the old `checkEnvironment.ts`
ran `typeof window`, `typeof process`, etc. on every platform, producing
wrong results in Electron, Cloudflare Workers, Deno, and React Native
(see [linked issues](#runtime-detection-issues) below). Per-platform
`env` modules use static constants that are compile-time truths.

- **Simpler testing** — the old test scripts required `dev-tool run
build-package` and `dev-tool run build-test` to manually resolve
polyfill suffixes before tests could run. With subpath imports, Vitest
resolves `#platform/*` modules natively via `resolve.conditions` — no
prebuild step needed:
  ```diff
-"test:browser": "npm run clean && dev-tool run build-package &&
dev-tool run build-test && dev-tool run test:vitest --no-test-proxy
--browser",
-"test:node": "dev-tool run build-test --no-browser-test && dev-tool run
test:vitest --no-test-proxy",
  +"test:browser": "dev-tool run test:vitest --no-test-proxy --browser",
  +"test:node": "dev-tool run test:vitest --no-test-proxy",
+"test:react-native": "dev-tool run test:vitest --no-test-proxy
--browser -- -c vitest.react-native.config.ts",
  ```

- **Less code** — where browser and RN share identical implementations
(no-ops, web APIs), a single `-web.mts` file replaces two duplicates,
following the pattern already established by `sha256-web.mts`,
`inspect-web.mts`, etc.

### What changed

**Platform modules** — platform-specific code extracted into modules
resolved via `#platform/*` entries in `package.json#imports`:

| Import | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `#platform/encoding` | UTF-8 encode/decode |
| `#platform/env` | environment constants (`isBrowser`, `isNodeLike`,
etc.) |
| `#platform/formData` | form data preparation |
| `#platform/policies` | platform-specific pipeline policies |
| `#platform/types` | platform type re-exports (`FormData`, `Blob`,
`File`, etc.) |
| `#platform/typeGuards` | `isNodeReadableStream` /
`isWebReadableStream` |

Each has a Node (`.ts`), browser (`.mts`), and react-native (`.mts`)
variant selected at build time by warp target conditions and at test
time by Vite's `resolve.conditions`.

**Config** — package-level tsconfigs moved to `config/` subdirectory,
extending shared configs from `eng/tsconfigs/`. ESLint updated to use
`config/tsconfig.lint.json` with `projectService: false`.

**Deleted** — `checkEnvironment.ts`, redundant browser/RN duplicate
files, old root-level tsconfigs (`tsconfig.src.json`,
`tsconfig.test.json`, `tsconfig.test.node.json`,
`tsconfig.browser.config.json`, `tsconfig.samples.json`,
`tsconfig.snippets.json`).

### Runtime detection issues

The migration structurally eliminates a class of recurring bugs caused
by runtime environment sniffing:

**Electron** — both `isBrowser` and `isNodeLike` true; bundler served
browser files that threw on Node-only policies:
- #12983, #14025, #21110, #30123

**Cloudflare / Deno / edge** — `nodejs_compat` flags set
`process.versions.node`, triggering Node-only policies the runtime
doesn't support:
- #27077, #28672, #37345 (open — user workaround patches
`process.versions.node = undefined`)

**React Native** — `process` shims made `isNodeLike=true`, selecting
Node code paths that crash:
- #20787, #26609, #30411

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: add minLen prefilter to @pm operator (#1601)

* perf: add minLen prefilter to @pm operator

Compute the length of the shortest pattern at operator init time and
skip the Aho-Corasick automaton entirely when the input is shorter —
no pattern can possibly match.

This mirrors the minMatchLength prefilter already used by @rx. For
@pm the computation is trivial (min of literal lengths) vs @rx which
walks the regexp AST.

Applies to all @pm variants: pm, pmFromFile, pmFromDataset.

Micro-benchmark (10 patterns, Apple M2):
  below_minLen:    2ns/op, 0 allocs  (was ~98ns with Aho-Corasick)
  at_minLen:      98ns/op, 4 allocs  (unchanged — hits automaton)

CRS benchmark (benchstat, count=6):
  Memory:      -3.04% geomean (p=0.015)
  Allocations: -1.87% geomean (p=0.002)
  Time:        -2.92% geomean

* fix: minPatternLen returns 0 on empty pattern to disable incorrect short-circuit

commit.message:
Fix CREATE MACRO parameter list formatting (#43)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Implements the two formatting changes described in #32.

**ON/USING inline** — join keyword, table ref, alias, and condition are
all on one line. No more `\n ON` break.

**Alias alignment** — when all `FROM`/`JOIN` entries in a block are
simple table refs, aliases are padded so they start at the same column.
The column is `max(keyword_width + table_name_width) + 1`. If any entry
uses a subquery, alignment is skipped for the whole block but ON stays
inline.

**`AS` omitted from table aliases** — the `AS` keyword is dropped
between table name and alias on `FROM`/`JOIN` lines. Column aliases in
`SELECT` lists are unchanged.

## Changes

- `src/jarify/generator.py` — new helpers (`_join_keyword`,
`_table_alias_str`, `_inline_on_sql`, `_table_ref_only_sql`,
`_compute_join_align_width`) and overrides for `join_sql`, `from_sql`;
`select_sql` now sets `_join_alias_col` before delegating to super
- `tests/fixtures/joins/aligned_joins.{input,expected}.sql` — new
fixture covering a multi-table aligned block
- Updated `bare_join`, `multi_join`, `real_world`, and `basic_cte`
expected fixtures for inline ON and dropped `AS`
- `tests/test_formatter.py` — new `TestJoinFormatting` class (6 unit
tests)
- `docs/sql-style-guide.md` — updated "Table aliases" rule; added "JOIN
formatting" section

Resolves #32

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: apply user replacements inside /__sd__/ paths for consistent client URLs

Previously, withExternalURLsProtected shielded all /__sd__/<host> segments
from user-defined replacements (rep.ToAlias). This caused URL mismatches
on pages that use client-side routing: the browser navigated to e.g.
/__sd__/copilot.msctf.com/ but the server-rendered HTML referenced all
resources and canonical URLs as copilot.microsoft.com, making TanStack
Router's invariant check fail ("Invariant failed" / __isServerError).

The shield was added as a safety measure, but the director already applies
rep.ToOriginal to every inbound request path, correctly restoring the real
upstream hostname for routing regardless of what alias the client URL uses.
The protection was therefore unnecessary and actively harmful.

Fix: remove the subdomainPathRe pre-shielding step from
withExternalURLsProtected. After this change, /__sd__/copilot.microsoft.com
becomes /__sd__/copilot.msctf.com in responses (user replacement applied),
and the director's rep.ToOriginal restores it to /__sd__/copilot.microsoft.com
for upstream routing. Pages with client-side routers now see a consistent
alias URL everywhere and mount correctly.

Update tests to assert the new (correct) behavior: /__sd__/ host segments
ARE replaced by user replacements, matching the rest of the response body.

Note: access copilot.microsoft.com cleanly by targeting it directly:
  maskproxy -target copilot.microsoft.com -replace microsoft:msctf
rather than via /__sd__/ from -target microsoft.com, as TanStack Router
expects the app to be rooted at / not /__sd__/copilot.msctf.com/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: dynamic scope-based tool discovery (#21)

* feat: dynamic scope-based tool discovery

Add a tool registry system where tools are dynamically enabled/disabled
based on OAuth scopes granted during login. All tools start disabled
(except login and auth_status) and become visible after authentication.

Core changes:
- Add GraphScope enum and scope definitions (src/scopes.ts)
- Add tool registry with defineTool(), syncToolState(), buildInstructions()
- Extend Authenticator interface with grantedScopes() method
- MsalAuthenticator reads scopes from config, caches granted scopes
- Config extended with optional selectedScopes field
- createMcpServer() is now async, generates dynamic instructions
- Tools use MCP SDK RegisteredTool.enable()/disable() + sendToolListChanged()

Login page scope flyout:
- Cog icon opens a CSS flyout with scope checkboxes
- User.Read and offline_access always checked and disabled
- POST /save-scopes persists scope selection to config
- POST /restart-login triggers ScopeChangeError for login retry
- Dark mode support via design tokens

Tool scope mapping:
- Always enabled: login, auth_status
- User.Read: logout
- Mail.Send: mail_send
- Tasks.Read OR Tasks.ReadWrite: todo_list, todo_show, todo_steps, todo_config
- Tasks.ReadWrite: todo_create, todo_update, todo_complete, todo_delete,
  todo_add_step, todo_update_step, todo_delete_step

New files: src/scopes.ts, src/tool-registry.ts
New tests: test/scopes.test.ts, test/tool-registry.test.ts,
  test/integration/dynamic-tools.test.ts, test/loopback-scopes.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove Tasks.Read scope (not yet supported)

Remove GraphScope.TasksRead enum value and all references. Only
Tasks.ReadWrite is currently supported. Tasks.Read can be added
back when read-only task access is implemented.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use wslview to open browser in WSL environments

Detect WSL by reading /proc/version for 'microsoft' or 'WSL' markers.
When running under WSL, use wslview (from wslu) instead of xdg-open
to open the host Windows browser. Detection is cached after first call.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: simplify scope handling — use .default, remove flyout

Entra returns all previously-consented scopes regardless of what's
requested, making the scope selection UI and config persistence
unnecessary.

Changes:
- Use .default scope for MSAL acquireTokenInteractive and
  acquireTokenSilent instead of loading selected scopes from config
- Remove login page scope flyout (cog icon, checkboxes, JS)
- Remove /save-scopes and /restart-login loopback endpoints
- Remove ScopeChangeError and retry loop from MsalAuthenticator.login()
- Remove selectedScopes from Config schema and related helpers
- Remove SCOPE_FLYOUT_STYLE from template styles
- Remove LoginLoopbackClient configDir parameter
- Delete test/loopback-scopes.test.ts (tested removed functionality)
- Update test/config.test.ts (remove selectedScopes tests)

Dynamic tool enable/disable still works — reads granted scopes from
the MSAL token response after login.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: make logout always visible regardless of granted scopes

Logout is a local session management operation (clears cache files) —
it doesn't need any Graph API scopes. Making it scope-gated meant it
could be hidden when the startup auth check failed, leaving the user
logged in but unable to log out.

Changes:
- Change LOGOUT_DEF.requiredScopes from [UserRead] to [] (always enabled)
- Add auth guard in logout handler: returns helpful message when not
  logged in instead of attempting to clear nonexistent tokens
- Update integration tests to expect logout in always-visible tool list

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: request User.Read scope instead of .default for MSAL

The .default scope causes Entra to return fully-qualified scope strings
with the Microsoft Graph resource GUID prefix (e.g.
00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000/Mail.Send), which don't match our
short-form GraphScope enum values. This caused toGraphScopes() to filter
out all scopes, leaving no tools enabled.

Requesting User.Read explicitly causes Entra to return all consented
scopes in short-form (Mail.Send, Tasks.ReadWrite, etc.) that match our
enum values correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: include arch in macOS updater bundle filenames

Both aarch64 and x86_64 builds produced 'HiveMind OS.app.tar.gz' with
the same name. When uploaded to the same GitHub release, one overwrote
the other, but latest.json contained per-arch signatures — causing
'signature verification failed' for whichever arch got overwritten.

Now renames to HiveMind OS_aarch64.app.tar.gz and
HiveMind OS_x86_64.app.tar.gz so both coexist in the release.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add synthetics_stats_overview panel support to elasticstack_kibana_dashboard (#2165)

* feat: add synthetics_stats_overview panel support to kibana_dashboard

Adds the `synthetics_stats_overview_config` typed block for
`type = "synthetics_stats_overview"` panels, with optional display
settings (title, description, hide_title, hide_border), URL drilldowns,
and per-category Synthetics monitor filters (projects, tags, monitor_ids,
locations, monitor_types).

All fields are optional; an absent or empty config block produces a valid
panel showing statistics for all monitors. Null-preservation is applied on
read-back so optional fields omitted by the practitioner do not drift.

Closes tasks 2.1–2.9 and 3.1–3.7 of the synthetics-stats-overview-panel
OpenSpec change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: simplify synthetics stats overview panel converters

Eliminate repeated anonymous-struct boilerplate in the filter read/write
paths by using a local type alias (type apiFilterItem = struct{...}) and

instead of the anonymous Config field to avoid repeating the large
anonymous struct signature in every function parameter list.

The Kibana 9.4.0-SNAPSHOT API rejects statuses as an unexpected property
in the synthetics_stats_overview filters. The schema/model/unit tests retain
the statuses dimension (satisfying REQ-033 at the provider schema level), but

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove trigger/type from synthetics_stats_overview drilldown schema

Aligns with other panel drilldown patterns (slo_overview_config,
slo_error_budget_config) by hardcoding trigger=SINGLE_CLICK and
type=URL_DRILLDOWN in the write converter instead of exposing them
as user-configurable fields. Removes duplicate syntheticsFilterItemModel
declaration (already defined in models_synthetics_monitors_panel.go).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: align REQ-033 spec with hardcoded drilldowns and fix empty-filters read-back

- Update REQ-033 delta spec: remove trigger/type as user-configurable
  drilldown fields; document they are hardcoded constants (matching
  slo_overview_config / slo_error_budget_config pattern)
- Fix readSyntheticsStatsOverviewFiltersFromAPI: distinguish nil API
  filters (field absent → preserve prior) from explicit empty object
  (→ set null in state), matching the spec's Read-back with empty

- Add unit tests for both filter cases on the refresh path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: nil out synthetics_stats_overview_config block on refresh when API returns empty config

When all API config fields are nil (including cfg.Filters == nil), the config block
now round-trips as null in state on refresh, matching the import-path behaviour
required by REQ-033. The prior "preserve filters" behaviour is retained only when
Kibana returns other config fields alongside nil filters.

Replaces the incorrect refresh_nilFilters_preservesPrior test (which was asserting
wrong behaviour) with two tests that together cover the corrected semantics:
- allNilConfig_nilsBlock: empty API config → block nil'd
- nilFiltersWithOtherConfig_preservesPriorFilters: nil filters + other config → filters preserved

commit.message:
fix: trim SSE history to last-per-notification-type

TrackingSseEventStreamStore._history now removes previous entries
of the same notification method before adding a new one. This keeps
history bounded (at most one selection_changed + one diagnostics_changed)
while preserving non-notification messages for SSE reconnection replay.

Adds 12 unit tests covering trimming behavior, mixed message types,
channel passthrough, and edge cases.

commit.message:
fix(open-agent): switch default model to llama3.2:3b for working tool calls (#30)

qwen2.5-coder models (7b and 14b-instruct) do not return proper tool_calls
via Ollama's OpenAI-compat layer — they output the tool invocation as raw
JSON text in the content field, so Pi never executes any tools.

llama3.2:3b returns finish_reason: tool_calls with a populated tool_calls
array, which is what Pi expects. Verified end-to-end: Pi reads files and
runs bash tools correctly.

Changes:
- .pi/settings.json: defaultModel llama3.2:3b, updated enabledModels
- config/models.json: add llama3.2:3b as first/default, keep qwen as secondary
  with notes that tools are broken for them
- setup.sh: pull llama3.2:3b first, qwen2.5-coder:7b as secondary
- README.md: update architecture diagram, prereqs, setup steps, troubleshooting

Closes ANT-35

Co-authored-by: antonclaesson <anton.claesson@hotmail.se>
